planning_specialization.description: Three planning specialization electives; at least two at 300-level or above

electives.description: Four additional electives from CRP/PPP/UPD course offerings

constraints: EXPOS101 or equivalent writing placement required as a prerequisite.
EJBSC205 or an approved statistics equivalent satisfies the statistics prerequisite.
CRP400 internship requires 64+ earned credits and CRP200.
At least 18 credits must be at the 300-level or above.

electives.description: Two additional Health Administration electives at 300-level or above

constraints: EXPOS101 or equivalent required as a prerequisite.
EJBSC205 or equivalent statistics course required.
EJBSC499 internship requires completion of EJBSC395 or PUBH395 and EJBSC450 and 100+ credits.
Total major credits: approximately 55.

prerequisites.description: Required prerequisites (not counted toward major credits)
notes: One human biology course (3-4 cr): e.g. BIO115 or equivalent.
One chemistry course (3-4 cr): CHEM127, CHEM128, CHEM161, or CHEM162.

electives.description: 15 credits of PUBH electives; at least 9 credits at 300-level or above

constraints: PUBH212 is required for majors; may be waived for transfer students with equivalent course.
PUBH395 requires EJBSC205 or equivalent.
PUBH450 requires 85+ earned credits and co-registration with PUBH395.
PUBH499 requires PUBH395, PUBH450, and 100+ earned credits.
Total major credits: 46-47.

electives.description: 12 credits of PPOL electives, up to 6 credits from other Bloustein courses, up to 6 credits from outside Bloustein

constraints: EXPOS101 or equivalent required as a prerequisite.
EJBSC499 internship requires EJBSC395, EJBSC450, and 100+ credits.
At least 12 elective credits must be from PPOL-coded courses.
Total major credits: approximately 40-46.

electives.description: Five upper-level electives (15 credits) from approved UPD/CRP/PPP list

constraints: EXPOS101 or equivalent required as a prerequisite.
UPD404 (Studio I) requires UPD315 and UPD316.
UPD405 (Studio II) requires UPD404.
Total major credits: approximately 55.

electives.description: Five additional CRP/PPP elective courses (15 credits); four must be at 300-level or above

constraints: 18 total credits required.
CRP200 (or PPP200) is the gateway course.
At least 12 credits must be at the 300-level or above.

electives.description: Three upper-level HA courses (300-level or above)

constraints: 18 total credits required.
Students majoring in Health Administration may not also pursue this minor.

electives.description: Five PUBH elective courses (15 credits); at least three at 300-level or 400-level

constraints: 24 total credits required.
Courses designated for majors only (PUBH212, PUBH395, PUBH450, PUBH499) are excluded from the minor.
Students majoring in Public Health may not also pursue this minor.

electives.description: Five Bloustein elective courses (15 credits); at least four at 300-level or above

constraints: 18 total credits required.
Students majoring in Public Policy may not also pursue this minor.

electives.description: Three upper-level UPD/CRP/PPP elective courses (9 credits) at 300-level or above

constraints: 21 total credits required.
Students majoring in Urban Planning and Design may not also pursue this minor.

field_experience_requirement.description: One field experience or practicum course (3 credits)
notes: Contact Bloustein undergraduate office for approved field experience options.

constraints: 18 total credits required.
DISA265 is the gateway course (37:575:265 Disability Studies: Intro to the Field).
Students complete a field experience component in addition to coursework.

core_requirement.description: Two core health equity courses (6 credits)

track_requirement.description: Two courses from one approved track: Food Justice, Housing Justice, or Education Justice (6 credits)

electives.description: Two additional elective courses from either core or track lists (6 credits)

constraints: 18 total credits required.
At least 9 credits must be at the 300-level or above.

sas_electives.description: 6 credits of SAS philosophy or biology electives

additional_requirement.description: 3 additional credits from either SAS or Bloustein elective lists above

constraints: 21 total credits required.
MEHE400 (Research Ethics) requires MEHE201 as a prerequisite.
Students may not double-count more than one course with their major.

electives.description: Two Bloustein elective courses (6 credits); at least one at 300-level or above

constraints: 18 total credits required.
PADM209 is the gateway course.
Open to students in any Rutgers school.

electives.description: Five elective courses (15 credits); at least four at 300-level or above. Courses may be drawn from Bloustein, SAS Sociology, Economics, Political Science, or other approved departments.

constraints: 18 total credits required.
URST101 is the required gateway course.
Consult Bloustein undergraduate advising for the full list of approved electives across departments.

mcrp.description: The MCRP is an AICP-accredited professional degree preparing students for careers in urban and regional planning. Students gain skills in planning theory, law, quantitative and qualitative methods, GIS, and urban economics, then apply them in two studio experiences. Concentrations available in: land use and environmental planning, transportation, community development, housing, international development, and real estate.

studio.description: Two planning studios (12 credits); Studio I requires core methods and theory

electives.description: Five graduate electives from the approved MCRP list (15 credits); at least three must be CRP-coded

capstone.description: Master's Research Project (3 credits) — completed in the final semester

constraints: CRP510 (Planning Studio I) requires completion of CRP501 and CRP502.
CRP511 (Planning Studio II) requires completion of CRP510.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 is required to remain in good academic standing.
Students must demonstrate writing proficiency; a writing sample is required at admission.
Total: 18 (core) + 12 (studios) + 15 (electives) + 3 (capstone) = 48 credits.
dual_degrees: MCRP/MPP — complete both degrees in three years (72 credits combined)
MCRP/MPH — complete both degrees in three years (75 credits combined)
JD/MCRP — offered jointly with Rutgers Law School
notes: AICP accredited; graduates eligible to sit for AICP exam after two years of professional practice.

mpa.description: The NASPAA-accredited MPA prepares students for leadership roles in government, nonprofits, and public-service organizations. The curriculum covers public management, policy analysis, finance, law, research methods, and organizational behavior. Students select a concentration in public management, nonprofit management, or health administration.

electives.description: Six graduate electives from the approved MPA list (18 credits); at least four must be from a single concentration

concentrations.description: Students select one concentration (at least 4 electives must come from concentration list)

constraints: PADM599 (MPA Capstone Seminar) must be completed in the final semester and requires completion of all core courses.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 is required to remain in good academic standing.
Students entering without prior public-sector coursework may be required to complete a leveling module.
Total: 24 (core) + 18 (electives) = 42 credits.
dual_degrees: MPA/MPP — complete both degrees in three years (63 credits combined)
MPA/MPH — complete both degrees in three years (69 credits combined)

mph.description: The CEPH-accredited MPH develops public health professionals skilled in epidemiology, biostatistics, health policy, environmental health, and social-behavioral sciences. Students complete a supervised practicum and an integrative learning experience. Concentrations available in: epidemiology, health policy and management, social and behavioral health, environmental health, and global health.

required_core.description: Seven required core courses covering the five foundational areas of public health (21 credits)

practicum.description: Supervised field practicum in an approved public health setting (3 credits); must be completed before the Integrative Learning Experience

electives.description: Seven graduate electives from the approved MPH concentration list (21 credits); at least four must be from the chosen concentration

concentrations.description: Students select one concentration (at least 4 electives must come from concentration list)

constraints: PUBH511 (Integrative Learning Experience) requires prior completion of PUBH510 (Practicum).
PUBH510 practicum placement requires completion of at least 24 credits including all core courses.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 is required to remain in good academic standing.
Students must document 200 hours of fieldwork for PUBH510.
Total: 21 (core) + 3 (practicum) + 21 (electives) = 45 credits.
dual_degrees: MPH/MCRP — complete both degrees in three years (75 credits combined)
MPH/MPA — complete both degrees in three years (69 credits combined)
MPH/MPP — complete both degrees in three years (69 credits combined)
notes: CEPH accredited; program satisfies prerequisites for many doctoral public health programs.

mpp.description: The MPP trains analysts and managers who can formulate, implement, and evaluate public policies across issue areas. Students develop rigorous quantitative and qualitative analytical skills, grounded in microeconomics and political science. Policy specialization tracks include: education policy, health policy, environmental policy, urban and housing policy, and social policy.

required_core.description: Seven required core courses covering foundations of policy analysis (21 credits)

capstone.description: Policy Capstone Seminar (3 credits) — taken in the final semester; students produce a policy analysis memo or report

electives.description: Six graduate electives from the approved MPP specialization list (18 credits); at least three must be from the chosen policy track

policy_tracks.description: Students select one policy track (at least 3 electives must come from track list)

constraints: PPOL509 (Capstone) must be taken in the final semester; requires completion of all seven core courses.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 3.0 is required to remain in good academic standing.
PPOL501 (Quantitative Methods) and PPOL502 (Microeconomics) must be completed before enrolling in most 500-level electives.
Total: 21 (core) + 3 (capstone) + 18 (electives) = 42 credits.
dual_degrees: MPP/MCRP — complete both degrees in three years (72 credits combined)
MPP/MPA — complete both degrees in three years (63 credits combined)
JD/MPP — offered jointly with Rutgers Law School
